title: Tenant / Workspace Boundary Decision

terms.definition: a logical boundary that isolates one customer's data, grants, evidence, audit records, and operator identities from another customer's
a scoped operating context within a tenant, typically mapping to a team, department, or organizational unit that shares grants and evidence but remains isolated from other workspaces
an authenticated individual or service identity that performs actions within a tenant/workspace boundary
a single, controlled deployment of GrantLayer used for evaluation, demonstration, or early partner validation
the scope within which a grant record, its rules, and its execution history are visible and manageable
the scope within which evidence files, verification records, and attestations are visible and manageable
the scope within which audit events, immutability chains, and tamper-evident records are visible and manageable

options_considered.name: single-tenant pilot only
options_considered.description: Run each pilot on a fully isolated deployment; no tenant/workspace concepts needed inside the application

options_considered.name: operator-bounded workspace model
options_considered.description: Run a single deployment where all data is implicitly bounded by the operator owner and their scope of control

options_considered.name: full multi-tenant SaaS model
options_considered.description: Implement explicit tenant/workspace identity, tenant-aware authorization, tenant-scoped data models, and cross-tenant access prevention

options_considered.name: defer tenant implementation but explicitly forbid multi-tenant claims
options_considered.description: Document that tenant/workspace isolation is not implemented and explicitly forbid multi-tenant claims

recommended_decision.near_term: controlled pilot may proceed under single-environment/operator-bounded assumptions
recommended_decision.before_production_saas: explicit tenant/workspace data and authorization boundary must be designed, implemented, and verified
recommended_decision.go_for: controlled pilot under Option A or Option B assumptions
recommended_decision.no_go_for: shared multi-tenant SaaS under Option C until follow-up issues are completed
recommended_decision.documented_under: Option D — no multi-tenant claims are permitted

explicit_non_claims: no shared SaaS environment approved for unrelated customers
pilot_go_criteria: single pilot environment or clearly bounded operator/workspace scope
pilot_no_go_criteria: tenant isolation is assumed but not implemented
